title: ipomoea polycarpa
intro: Ipomoea polycarpa, also known as the many-fruited morning glory, is a perennial vine plant native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Ipomoea polycarpa include its ability to treat various conditions such as fever, cough, and sore throats, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar levels. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by drying and grinding the leaves and roots, which can then be used to make teas, infusions, or poultices.
